one_line_takeaway: PriRFed is the first federated-learning framework for manifold-valued models with a formal end-to-end differential-privacy guarantee and matching convergence analysis, using tangent-space Gaussian noise in local training to drop the trusted-server assumption and a one-step 'tangent mean' to aggregate on the manifold.
key_finding: PriRFed provably attains (eps',delta')-DP end-to-end while converging on all three manifold problems. Table 1 shows the composed budget stays small for modest sampling: with per-round (eps,delta)=(0.15,1e-4) and hat-delta=1e-3, N=100/s=1/rho=0.01 gives eps'=0.0426 at T=50 rising only to 0.136 at T=500; a small sampling rate lets T grow large while eps',delta' stay controlled. Empirically, PriRFed-DP-RSVRG consistently converges faster than PriRFed-DP-RSGD and is less sensitive to the added noise (especially when local sample counts Ni are small, i.e., noise is larger). Increasing eps (less noise) makes cost values more accurate and shrinks the gradient norm, and increasing the number of agents N tightens the empirical convergence bound - both matching the theory. On WordNet hyperbolic embedding, the private predictions for the held-out word 'primate' converge close to the true pretrained embedding while preserving hierarchy and privacy (though RSVRG variants overfit and drift after reaching a good solution).
core_contribution: The paper proposes PriRFed, a generic federated-learning framework for models whose parameters live on a Riemannian manifold (e.g., the sphere, SPD matrices with the affine-invariant metric, the Lorentz hyperboloid) while providing differential-privacy (DP) guarantees. The authors state this is the first FL framework on Riemannian manifolds that simultaneously carries a formal privacy guarantee AND convergence results. Two design ideas are its own: (1) two-level privacy protection - agents run a privately-DP local-training procedure so the parameters they upload are already DP ('locality'), which removes the trusted-server assumption, and the composed global mechanism is also proven DP ('globality'); and (2) a manifold-appropriate aggregation called the 'tangent mean' - because the Euclidean weighted average (FedAvg) is not valid on a nonlinear manifold and the exact Frechet mean has no closed form, the server does one step of Riemannian steepest descent on the Frechet-mean objective (Eq. 3.2) to approximate it. The central theoretical result (Theorem 4.2) proves Algorithm 1 is (eps',delta')-DP by chaining pre-processing (c-stable transformation, Thm 4.1), subsampling amplification (Lemma 4.1), sequential composition (Thm 2.2), post-processing through the tangent mean (Thm 2.1), and advanced composition across T rounds (Thm 2.3). The framework is generic in its local DP procedure; the paper instantiates and analyzes convergence for two existing procedures, DP-RSGD and DP-RSVRG.
threat_model: An honest-but-curious or untrusted server plus an external adversary who can manipulate input datasets and probe the algorithm's output to infer sensitive information about individual agents (membership/record inference against FL). DP is used with RECORD-LEVEL adjacency: two datasets D, D' are adjacent if they differ by at most one data point (|D XOR D'| <= 2), as opposed to the user-level adjacency of DP-FedAvg. The key security improvement over prior FL+DP work (e.g., DP-FedSGD/DP-FedAvg add noise at the server) is that noise is injected during LOCAL training so the server never sees clean iterates - hence the trusted-server assumption is eliminated. Privacy is quantified by the (eps,delta)-DP budget; smaller eps means stronger privacy and larger noise variance, trading off against convergence quality.
limitations: PriRFed is the first-of-its-kind, so the experiments compare only its own private vs. non-private variants against a 'True Value' oracle - there is no external competing private-Riemannian-FL baseline (none exists), so the private cost of the method vs. alternatives is not benchmarked. The tangent-mean aggregation only approximately solves the Frechet-mean problem (single Riemannian gradient step). Analysis assumes all iterates and optima lie in a totally normal, compact neighborhood with bounded curvature, and requires geodesic Lipschitz/smoothness; the geodesic Lipschitz constant Lf is unknown in practice and is handled via gradient clipping. Convergence theorems for the K=1 nonconvex case (Thm 5.1) require all agents active with one-step full-batch descent. The composition bounds rely on the standard (not optimal, per KOV17/MV15) advanced-composition theorem. The paper is math/optimization-flavored (arXiv math.OC); privacy accounting uses classic composition rather than Renyi/moments accountant. Authors note room for more efficient local procedures and aggregation.

datasets_used: Synthetic covariance data via Wishart/Gaussian generation following [RBS21]/[HMJG24] (principal eigenvector on sphere; Frechet mean on SPD)
MNIST (60,000 28x28 images; d+1=784) for principal eigenvector on sphere manifold, Problem 6.1
PATHMNIST from MedMNIST [YSW+23] (89,996 RGB images; 20,000 sampled, each mapped to a 9x9 SPD covariance descriptor) for Frechet mean on SPD manifold, Problem 6.2
WordNet mammals subtree [Mil95] (n=1180 nodes, 6540 edges; Laplacian-eigenmap features r=3; hyperbolic embeddings on H2) for hyperbolic structured prediction, Problem 6.3
quantitative_results: Table 1, N=200,s=1,rho=0.005: eps'=0.0213 at T=50 up to 0.0676 at T=500 (smaller-budget than s=5 rows)
Table 1, N=100,s=5,rho=0.05: eps' grows much faster, 1.58 (T=50) to 6.03 (T=500), showing larger sampling inflates the budget
DP-RSGD noise scale for (eps,delta)-DP: sigma_i^2 = o_i * K log(1/delta) tau^2 / (Ni^2 eps^2) (HMJG24 Thm 1); DP-RSVRG uses sigma_i^2 = o~_i * mK log(1/delta) tau^2 /(Ni^2 eps^2)
Thm 5.1 (nonconvex, K=1) bound: min_{0<=t<=T} E[||grad f(x^t)||^2] <= (2/(T alpha))(f(x0)-f*) + (d Lg alpha/(sum Ni)^2) sum Ni^2 sigma_i^2

Experiment scale: MNIST scenarios (N,Ni) in {(50,1200),(60,1000),(80,750),(100,600)}; synthetic SPD (20,250),(50,100); PATHMNIST (50,400),(100,200); WordNet N=9,Ni=131; all results averaged over 10 runs
Privacy-level sweep on sphere/synthetic: eps in {0.08,0.15,0.3} with delta=1e-5 - larger eps yields lower cost and smaller gradient norm (Fig. 1)
baselines_compared: PriRFed-DP-RSGD (Algorithm 1 + DP-RSGD local training) vs PriRFed-DP-RSVRG (Algorithm 1 + DP-RSVRG local training) - the two instantiations analyzed
Non-private counterparts PriRFed-RSGD and PriRFed-RSVRG (WordNet experiment, Figs. 5-6)
'True Value' / optimal cost computed by Riemannian steepest descent (Manopt) as the convergence reference
Conceptual/related-work comparison (not run): user-level DP-FedSGD/DP-FedAvg [MRTZ17], NbAFL [WLD+20], PriFedSync [ZCLS21], and the non-private Riemannian-FL RFedSVRG [LM23] which lacks DP
